result:
Build is clean. The fix is complete.

**Root cause:** `emit_inst` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:560-574` (the Phase-A bootstrap codegen path used by most parser emission sites) computed `lineinfo[pc]` via `(line - previousline).clamp(i8::MIN, i8::MAX) as i8`. When the line delta was ≤ -128 (or in general exceeded i8 range), the clamp produced `i8::MIN = -128`, which is exactly the `ABSLINEINFO` sentinel. The function never pushed a matching `AbsLineInfo` entry to `f.abslineinfo` or maintained `iwthabs` / `nabslineinfo`, so `debug.get_func_line`'s walk later encountered an ABSLINEINFO byte with no abs entry behind it and panicked. With closure.lua's 272-line main chunk producing 865 lineinfo entries, several lineinfo[] bytes ended up as -128 with an empty `abslineinfo`.

**Fix:** Replaced the clamp with the same `savelineinfo` logic used in `lua-code/src/codegen.rs:701`: when `|linedif| >= LIMLINEDIFF (0x80)` or `iwthabs >= MAXIWTHABS (128)`, push an `AbsLineInfo { pc, line }` entry, bump `nabslineinfo`, write `ABS_LINE_INFO` to `lineinfo[pc]`, and reset `iwthabs = 1`; otherwise write the raw delta and increment `iwthabs`. Added an `AbsLineInfo` import from `lua_types`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. Did not re-run the program per instructions; the mega-loop will re-test.
